name: ethical-hacking-methodology
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "learn ethical hacking", "understand penetration testing lifecycle", "perform reconnaissance", "conduct security scanning", "exploit vulnerabilities", or "write penetration test reports". It provides comprehensive ethical hacking methodology and techniques.
capabilities: Guide users through the five phases of ethical hacking methodology
Provide command examples for standard security tools like nmap, Nikto, and Metasploit
Explain reconnaissance techniques including OSINT and passive information gathering
Outline vulnerability assessment and exploitation procedures for authorized testing
Define professional reporting structures for security findings
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill teaches authorized penetration testing methodology. Static findings flag standard security tools (nmap, Metasploit, nikto) and techniques that are used by professional security testers worldwide. The skill explicitly requires written authorization, defines clear scope boundaries, and includes ethical guidelines. Risk is mitigated by the authorization requirements and educational context.
valueStatement: Security professionals need a structured methodology for authorized penetration testing. This skill provides the complete pentesting lifecycle from reconnaissance to reporting, covering essential tools and techniques used by certified ethical hackers.
userTitle: Learn Ethical Hacking and Penetration Testing
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization from target system owner
Must stay within defined scope boundaries
Cannot be used for unauthorized systems or malicious purposes
Designed for educational and professional security testing contexts only

useCases.targetUser: Aspiring penetration testers and security analysts
useCases.title: Security Professional Learning Penetration Testing
useCases.description: New security professionals learning the structured methodology for authorized penetration testing engagements

useCases.title: Preparing for Security Certifications
useCases.description: Candidates preparing for CEH, OSCP, or other security certifications needing hands-on methodology reference

useCases.targetUser: Professional penetration testers and security consultants
useCases.title: Structured Security Assessment Workflow
useCases.description: Security consultants following a documented methodology for client penetration testing engagements

promptTemplates.title: Introduction to Ethical Hacking
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the five phases of ethical hacking methodology and what happens in each phase of a penetration test.
promptTemplates.scenario: Learning the fundamentals of penetration testing lifecycle

promptTemplates.title: Reconnaissance Techniques
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the passive reconnaissance techniques I should use before scanning a target system? Include examples of OSINT gathering.

promptTemplates.title: Scanning and Enumeration
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use nmap for port scanning and service enumeration. Include commands for TCP SYN, UDP, and service version detection.

promptTemplates.title: Exploitation and Reporting
promptTemplates.prompt: Walk me through the exploitation phase including how to use Metasploit, and explain the structure of a professional penetration testing report.

bestPractices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any system
Document every action taken during the penetration test
Follow the rules of engagement and stay within defined scope
antiPatterns: Testing systems without explicit written permission
Exceeding the defined scope of testing
Using discovered vulnerabilities for personal gain or unauthorized access

faq.question: Do I need permission to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is designed for authorized penetration testing only. You must have written permission from the system owner before testing any target.

faq.question: What tools does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill covers industry-standard tools including nmap, Nikto, Gobuster, Hydra, SQLMap, and Metasploit Framework.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: This skill requires basic networking knowledge and Linux command-line proficiency. It is designed for those preparing for security certifications or starting a career in penetration testing.

faq.question: What are the phases of ethical hacking?
faq.answer: The main phases are reconnaissance, scanning, vulnerability analysis, exploitation, maintaining access, and reporting.

faq.question: Can this skill be used for malicious purposes?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly requires authorization and includes ethical guidelines. Unauthorized access is illegal and strictly prohibited.

faq.question: What certification paths use this methodology?
faq.answer: This methodology aligns with certifications like CEH (Certified Ethical Hacker), OSCP (Offensive Security Certified Professional), and CompTIA PenTest+.
